verses.text: 'Praise God in his sanctuary: praise him in the firmament of his power,' for His mighty acts and excellent greatness. Heaven and earth are His temple of praise. Both His deeds and His being call forth our worship.

'Praise God in his sanctuary: praise him in the firmament of his power,' for His mighty acts and excellent greatness. Heaven and earth are His temple of praise. Both His deeds and His being call forth our worship.
Praise Him with trumpet, harp, timbrel, strings, and 'loud cymbals,' every instrument joining the song. No music is too much for His praise. The fullness of joy pours itself out in every sound.
Praise Him with trumpet, harp, timbrel, strings, and 'loud cymbals,' every instrument joining the song. No music is too much for His praise. The fullness of joy pours itself out in every sound.
Praise Him with trumpet, harp, timbrel, strings, and 'loud cymbals,' every instrument joining the song. No music is too much for His praise. The fullness of joy pours itself out in every sound.
'Let every thing that hath breath praise the LORD. Praise ye the LORD.' The Psalter ends with this universal call. Every creature that draws breath owes its life's last use to the praise of God.
